title: dianthus pungens var. multiaffinis
intro: Dianthus pungens var. multiaffinis, also known as the fragrant or piney pink, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is a perennial herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The leaves and flowers of Dianthus pungens var. multiaffinis can be made into herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
